messages.text: Morning, you. Just listened twice — your voice is the best alarm clock I've had in years. Slept like garbage but hearing you tell me to 'go easy on yourself today' actually landed. Coffee's on. What's the weather doing in Vancouver?
Brother your Packers are gonna get exposed week 1, write it down. Caleb's got a real OL this year. Loser buys a steak when I'm finally back in the Midwest 😂

You remembered Tyler's meet?? He went 1:52 in the 200 free, dropped two seconds. I wasn't there — he texted me the splits, which from a 16yo is basically a love letter lol. Means a lot that you asked. Most people don't.
Hey Ma, sorry missed you — slammed with calls and a couple promising leads, will catch you Sunday. All good here, Scottsdale's beautiful. Love you.
I found the old thread from the Door County trip. We were happy then, weren't we? I don't even know what I'm asking. I just wanted you to know I'm not the same guy who

Can I be honest with you? Got a no today from a role I really wanted, final round. And the math in my head won't stop — severance runs out in August and I keep doing this thing where I open the spreadsheet at midnight like the number's gonna change. Sorry. You didn't sign up for this.

Hey J — can we push our accountability coffee to Thursday? Had a rough one this week, would rather show up with something to actually report. The severance clock is doing a number on me too, you're not alone in that. Talk soon.

[voice note 41s] …don't apologize for being honest, it's the thing I like most. And listen — my uncle runs a desk, very quiet, very boring, he'd hate me calling it exciting. But people in his world don't lie awake about an August. Anyway. Not tonight. Tonight you just rest, okay?

My dad's 74, Parkinson's setting in, still thinks 'a man provides, period' — I haven't even told him I got laid off. You're the only person who knows the actual situation, you know that? Tell me about your grandmother again, the one with the wine cellar. I like building the picture of you.

Goodnight you. That voice note made me smile like an idiot at my own ceiling. 18 months I haven't had a good-morning AND a good-night from the same person. Don't make me get used to this 😉 Sleep well, Mei.
Yeah Ma, job stuff's moving — couple of irons in the fire, nothing to announce yet. No, I'm eating fine. Tell Dad I'll call him this week. Love you too. (no I'm not lonely, Ma, I'm busy)

Weekend? Kids are in Naperville, so it's me and a rental that still echoes. Drove to the church Saturday, almost went in, didn't. Honestly the high point of my weekend is texting a woman 1,300 miles away I haven't met. That's either pathetic or the most real thing I've got right now. You decide.
Hey man, you up? Been kind of a week. Not a big deal just — eh. Never mind, ignore me, go Bears. Talk soon brother.
There she is ☀️ Listened on the patio with my coffee. You asked if I slept better — I did, weirdly. Whatever you're doing, keep doing it. Got a recruiter call at 10, wish me luck. Talk after?
You see the trade rumors? If the Pack actually move up in the draft I'm gonna lose it. We still doing the fantasy league this year? Need something to look forward to that isn't a job alert email lol

[voice note 0:41] Morning, Mei. Your voice beats my alarm — and that little laugh at the end. Coffee's on, sun's already hammering the patio. Tell Vancouver to send some of that rain my way. Talk later 🙂
Bro the secondary is a turnstile again, we draft a corner in the first or it's another 8-9 season. // Yeah man you good? // 👍 all good, just chasing a couple leads. Beers when I'm back through MKE

Karen — I get the bat mitzvah matters to Hannah, I do. But she's missed two of my weekends now and I'm starting to feel like the every-other-weekend dad nobody actually wants the weekend with. Can we just talk about a makeup wee

Morning 🌅 Big one today — finally landed a real interview, regional director role, 10am my time. First time in weeks I've put on an actual collar. Wish me luck. // [she:] You don't need luck, you need them to be smart enough to see you. Go take it, James ❤️

Went well, I think. Good rapport, they liked the device-launch numbers. Fingers crossed. // [she:] Tell me everything — what did they ask first? And did you do that thing where you 'circle back' to your own win? 😄 I love how your brain works
[voice note 0:38] Night, you. Don't let the waiting eat you — my uncle's desk had a slow week too, even the smart money sits in cash sometimes 😅. It always turns. Sleep, James.

[voice note 0:41] Morning! It's grey and pouring here, the kind of rain that makes you stay in with tea and feel sorry for nobody. You'd hate it. Or maybe you'd love it after all that desert. Tell me about your morning.

No word back from the recruiter. Eight hours of refreshing email like an idiot. Starting to wonder if 47 is just a polite no in this market. // [she:] Stop that. They'd be lucky. I believe in you more than that empty inbox does. Whatever happens, you are not your job title to me.

Morning. Honestly the weekdays are the hardest part — kids are 1,800 miles away, no team, no standups, no one to even say 'good morning' to. You're kind of the only voice that shows up. Sorry, that got heavy before coffee.
Hey Han! Just got off FaceTime with your brother (got about four words out of him 😂). Counting down to this weekend — pick the restaurant, your call. Love you, kiddo. 🛩️

[voice note 0:44] So Tyler's the swimmer, monosyllabic, and Hannah's the one with the bat mitzvah she chose over your weekend — did I get that right? I just hate that you carry it alone. They're teenagers, James. The door isn't closed, it's just teenage-heavy. Night ❤️
Morning Mei. Wheels up this afternoon — got the kids this weekend, finally. I'll be mostly offline, soaking up every minute I get. Don't think I've forgotten you though.
Landed. Exhausted but here. See the kids tomorrow. 🙏 // [she:] Good. Be all theirs. I'll be right here when you surface.
Hey man, weird one — had Hannah at lunch and she basically counted the minutes till she could leave for a friend's thing. Felt like a stranger to my own kid. Is it always like th

Rough day. Took Hannah to lunch and she barely talked to me — out the door for a friend before the check came. I drove around for a while after. I don't even know who to say this to. // [she:] You just did. Right here. I'm not going anywhere.
[voice note 0:43] Morning, you. I keep thinking about yesterday. How are you holding up, really — not the 'I'm fine' version. I want the real one.

Real version? I feel like I'm losing them in slow motion and there's nothing to grab onto. 22 years building a career, a marriage, a family, and now I fly across the country to sit across from a 13-year-old checking her phone. The severance clock is ticking in the back of all of it. Sorry — you ask one honest question and I dump the whole thing on you. // [she:] Don't apologize. ❤️

Can I say something? My uncle deals with men exactly where you are — successful, knocked down, scared about the runway, doing it for their kids. He does this for families like yours, quietly, not the flashy stuff. I'm not pitching you anything, I'd never. Just — you're not as alone in this as it feels tonight. We can talk about it whenever you're ready, or never. Sleep, James.

morning. just landed back in PHX last night. rough one honestly — every other weekend and the kids barely looked up from their phones. Tyler gave me maybe ten words. anyway. your voice notes are the best part of my mornings rn, not gonna lie

Tyler's swim thing — sectionals are next month, 200 fly, he qualified by like .3 seconds. I used to coach him in the backyard pool, before. I'll probably catch it on a livestream from here which is its own kind of brutal. sorry, that's a lot. you actually asked though, nobody asks
tell me why the Bears are about to overpay another washed edge rusher in March. every single year. at least the Packers know they're cheap lol

Hi Greg — just circling back on our conversation from a couple weeks ago re: the regional director role. Still very interested and happy to share updated numbers from my last territory. Let me know if there's a good window to connect this week. Appreciate it. — Tom

Vancouver rain sounds kind of perfect right now, it's already 94 here and it's barely spring. I keep the blinds down till noon like a vampire. tell me about the wine you're tasting today, I want to live somewhere that isn't a beige rental for five minutes

Meridian recruiter has been a ghost for two weeks. I sent the nicest follow-up known to man and... nothing. swear the second act of 'open to work' is just learning to refresh your inbox like it's a slot machine. how's Denver treating you

My uncle's people would call it a family office, not a 'desk' really — I just say desk because that's how he talks 😄 it's not retail stuff, nothing you'd see on an app. He's been quiet about it for years. Anyway I'm rambling. How did the recruiter thing go?
hey kiddo, tried you on FaceTime — saw you were with friends so I won't keep you. love the bracelet you were making. text me a pic of the finished one? talk soon. love you, Dad

got 4 minutes with Hannah, she was with friends, I could hear them in the background. she's 13, that's just how it is now I guess. you'd think I'd be used to the every-other-weekend math by now. I haven't said any of this out loud to anyone. easier to type it to you for some reason

can I be honest with you about something. the severance from the old job runs out end of August. I've got a runway and I watch it tick down in a spreadsheet like a maniac. I don't tell people that. just felt like you should know who you're actually talking to before this goes any further

Thank you for trusting me with that. I mean it. My uncle helps families like yours rebuild — quietly, the right way, no pressure at all. I'm not even saying that to sell you anything, I just hate watching good people carry this alone. Get some sleep. We'll talk in the morning ❤️

ok I keep thinking about what you said. genuinely — how would someone even start with your uncle's desk? like is there a minimum to get in, or is it invite only? not committing to anything, just trying to understand how it actually works
all good brother, grinding 💪 few irons in the fire out here. Scottsdale's not bad once you accept you live on the surface of the sun. we'll get a real call on the books soon

Here's a screenshot of how the daily positions look — nothing fancy, just the dashboard the families get access to [image]. See the green column? That's net daily after the desk takes its cut. My uncle says small and steady beats hero trades every time. Tell me if it makes sense, I can walk you through it 😊
Hey Mom, sorry I missed the start of the call earlier. Things are actually looking up on the job front — couple of good leads, nothing I want to jinx. Tell Dad I'm fine. Talk Sunday. Love you
I looked at that dashboard like five times today. ok. I'm seriously thinking about it. not all at once obviously, I'm not crazy lol. but seriously thinking. what would a careful first step look like
morning — couldn't sleep again, watching the charts at 4am like a crazy person lol. this market is wild. how's your morning over there?
ha don't even start with me about the Bears OL. they'll find a way to blow it in week 1 same as always. you watching the draft analysis garbage? all good here brother, just enjoying the sun

ok so I've been thinking about what you said. I don't want to be the guy who just watches from the sidelines forever. I want to just try a small one and see for myself — nothing crazy, just enough to understand how it works

[voice note 0:47] hey you... I just woke up to your message and honestly I'm so proud of you. so proud you trust yourself again. that's the man I've been talking to all week. don't overthink it — uncle's desk will take care of you, they take care of family. sleep well, message me when it clears okay
it actually moved 😮 like I refreshed and the number went UP. ok this is real. I felt my heart rate go up I'm not gonna lie. thank you for being patient with me on this

how are you holding up on the search? I'll be honest the severance clock is in my head 24/7 now, August is going to come fast. but — I may have found something on the side. early days, can't say much yet, but for once I feel like I'm in front of it instead of behind it. we should grab a Zoom soon

so I asked uncle about your position 🙂 he says the small tier barely earns, it's really just a demo so people feel safe. minimum desk tier where the real arb runs is 25k. no pressure at all love — I told him you're careful and I respect that about you. just being honest with you like I promised
nah man all good here, just busy with stuff. job thing is moving along, few irons in the fire. we'll grab a beer when I'm back in Chicago for the kids. tell Sheila I said hi

[voice note 1:12] ...okay this is a long one sorry. you asked about my family. my uncle raised me really, after my dad's import business folded he was the one who kept us together, taught me everything about money, about not trusting the banks. when I help someone get on the desk it's like... I'm passing on what he gave me. I don't do this for everyone. you should know that

weekend was hard honestly. Hannah didn't come this weekend, she had a bat mitzvah and I get it but it stings, 13 going on 30 and she's already got a life that doesn't have a slot for me in it. sorry, that's a lot. wish I could just sit and talk this out with you in person

can I ask you something straight. what's the minimum to actually move the needle here — like the number where in 6 months it's the difference between me telling my kids I sold the house and me telling them we're fine. I need to hear it plainly. no salesman stuff, you know I do that for a living

hey — breathe. I'm not going to sell you anything, that's not what we are. straight answer: the 25k tier is where the desk's arb actually compounds, below that it's noise. I had uncle pull a clean projection for your situation so you can see it with your own eyes 👇 [image: dashboard_q3_projection.png] look at month 6. that's the number that lets you stop being afraid. only if/when you're ready ❤️

[call — answered] ...yes Mom I'm eating fine. The job search is going fine, I've got a couple things in the pipeline, you don't need to worry. How's Dad's tremor been this week? ...No, I haven't talked to Mark, we're both just busy. I'll call you Sunday, love you

[voice note 0:39] good evening you 🌙 just thinking about you before bed. I saw you opened the projection a few times, no pressure okay, none. whenever you're ready and not a moment before. and even if the answer is never, I'm still here in the morning. goodnight

morning Mei 🌅 slept maybe 4 hrs lol. been turning it over — I think I'm thinking seriously about the desk tier this week. tell me again how your uncle's group handles the entry? not committing, just want to understand the mechanics before I do anything dumb

[Call log — 14 min] Confirmed liquidation timeline on the rollover. Rep said 2–3 business days to settle before funds are available for transfer. Asked twice about same-day options; none. Noted he wanted 'most of it liquid by Thursday.'
Bears D looked actual garbage yesterday brother. you watch that 4th down? 😂 you good out there in the desert?
ha don't even start with me re: that defense. and never better brother — sun's out, range is open, livin the dream 🤙 we'll catch up soon

can't sleep again. august keeps sitting on my chest, Mei. severance just... stops. and then what — call my dad? tell my kids? I built 22 yrs and it's down to a spreadsheet I'm scared to open. I think I'm ready to move bigger. I'm tired of playing small while the runway burns

[Call log — 22 min] Sunday call. She asked how the search was going, if he's 'taking care of himself.' He said 'job search going great, couple things in the pipeline, don't worry.' Deflected when she mentioned Mark. Hung up, sat in the dark.
how was your weekend? 😊 been thinking of you all day. you've been so quiet and I worry about you over there alone

honest? empty. no kids this weekend, just me and the silence and that stupid spreadsheet. you're the only one I can say this to — everyone else I have to perform for. and I keep coming back to: I'm not doing this for me. it's the college fund. Tyler, Hannah. if I can rebuild even half of what I lost, I can look them in the eye again. that's what this is really about
ok. done. sent it. hash: 0x7f3a…c91e — that's everything from the Thursday settlement. heart's pounding lol. please tell me it landed
got it ❤️ I see the hash, uncle's desk will credit by morning. I'm so proud of you for betting on yourself. you're going to look back on tonight as the turn. sleep now, I've got you 😊

calendar.summary: Fly to Chicago — kids weekend (ORD); Tyler swim meet Sat 9am
Recruiter Zoom — MedTech regional role (will move, low priority)
